description: Indicateurs sport-science qui validatent l'effet du plan : aerobic decoupling, pace Z2/Z4-Z5, HRV CV, hill efficiency, FC vs pente, climb rate à FC fixée.

panels.title: EF Average 30d
panels.description: Efficiency Factor (GAP/HR). Plus haut = mieux. Indicateur Daniels d'efficacite aerobie.

panels.title: Pace Z2 medianne 30j
panels.description: Allure mediane quand HR ∈ Z2 (auto from InfluxDB HRZones). Plus rapide a FC constante = endurance s'ameliore. Filtre Speed ∈ [1.5, 6] m/s.

targets.query: SELECT 1000.0 / mean("Speed") AS "pace" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "HeartRate" >= $z2_hr AND "HeartRate" <= $z3_hr AND "Speed" >= 1.5 AND "Speed" <= 6 GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: HRV CV (14j)
panels.description: Coefficient de variation HRV. Stable (<10%) = bon. Eleve (>15%) = stress systeme nerveux.

targets.query: SELECT 100 * stddev("avgOvernightHrv") / mean("avgOvernightHrv") AS "CV %" FROM "SleepSummary" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(14d) fill(null)

panels.title: Avg HR at High Altitude
panels.description: FC moyenne quand altitude > 200m (= portions hautes des cotes locales).

targets.query: SELECT mean("HeartRate") AS "HR" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "Altitude" >= 200 GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Avg Climb Rate 30d
panels.description: Vitesse verticale moyenne par run (m/h). Plus haut = plus de travail vertical.

targets.query: SELECT ("elevationGain" / "elapsedDuration") * 3600 AS "m/h" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "elevationGain" > 30 AND "elapsedDuration" > 600

panels.title: Avg Decoupling %
panels.description: Drift EF: (1ere moitie - 2eme moitie) / 1ere moitie * 100. <5%=bonne base, >8%=trop rapide.

targets.query: SELECT median("RunningEfficiency") AS "ef_first" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "DurationSeconds" < 1800 GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

SELECT median("RunningEfficiency") AS "ef_after" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "DurationSeconds" >= 1800 AND "DurationSeconds" < 5400 GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Aerobic Decoupling : EF 1ere vs 2eme moitie de chaque run
panels.description: EF mediane sur les 30 premieres min vs les 30-90 min. Si la 2eme moitie chute = endurance pas assez solide. Cible : ecart < 5%.

targets.query: SELECT median("RunningEfficiency") AS "EF 1ere moitie (0-30min)" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "DurationSeconds" < 1800 AND "RunningEfficiency" > 0 GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

SELECT median("RunningEfficiency") AS "EF 2eme moitie (30-90min)" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "DurationSeconds" >= 1800 AND "DurationSeconds" < 5400 AND "RunningEfficiency" > 0 GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: 2. Pace progression hebdo (Z2 base aérobie + Z4-Z5 intensité)
Pace Z2 + Volume Z2 — hebdo Lun→Dim (barres côte à côte)
panels.description: Bucket hebdo Lun→Dim TZ Europe/Paris (la semaine en cours est incluse, partielle). Barre violette = pace moyen Z2 pondéré seconde par seconde (axe gauche, dthms, plus bas = mieux). Barre grise = volume Z2 en minutes (axe droit, fiabilité du point pace). Filtre Speed ∈ [1.5, 6] m/s pour exclure marche/spikes GPS. Cible : -10 sec/km sur 8 semaines à FC équivalente. Repères volume : <30 min/sem = bruité (ignorer le pace), 30-60 = correct, >60 = robuste.

targets.query: SELECT 1000.0 / mean("Speed") AS "Pace Z2 hebdo (pondérée)" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "HeartRate" >= $z2_hr AND "HeartRate" <= $z3_hr AND "Speed" >= 1.5 AND "Speed" <= 6 GROUP BY time(7d, 4d) fill(null) tz('Europe/Paris')

SELECT count("HeartRate") / 60.0 AS "Volume Z2 (min)" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "HeartRate" >= $z2_hr AND "HeartRate" <= $z3_hr GROUP BY time(7d, 4d) fill(0) tz('Europe/Paris')

panels.title: Pace Z4+Z5 + Volume Z4+Z5 — hebdo Lun→Dim (barres côte à côte)
panels.description: Bucket hebdo Lun→Dim TZ Europe/Paris (la semaine en cours est incluse, partielle). Barre rouge = pace moyen Z4+Z5 (HR ≥ 166) pondéré seconde par seconde (axe gauche, dthms, plus bas = mieux). Barre grise = volume Z4+Z5 en minutes (axe droit, fiabilité du point pace). Filtre Speed ∈ [1.5, 7] m/s. Sert à suivre la progression VO2max/seuil. Volume Z4+Z5 attendu : 5-15 min/sem en C1 (base), 15-40 min/sem en C2/C3 (intensité).

targets.query: SELECT 1000.0 / mean("Speed") AS "Pace Z4+Z5 hebdo (pondérée)" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "HeartRate" >= 166 AND "HeartRate" <= 195 AND "Speed" >= 1.5 AND "Speed" <= 7 GROUP BY time(7d, 4d) fill(null) tz('Europe/Paris')

SELECT count("HeartRate") / 60.0 AS "Volume Z4+Z5 (min)" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "HeartRate" >= 166 AND "HeartRate" <= 195 GROUP BY time(7d, 4d) fill(0) tz('Europe/Paris')

panels.title: HRV nuit (ms) + CV% (stabilite)
panels.description: HRV brute en bleu (gauche). CV% en rouge (droite) = ecart-type/moyenne. CV bas = systeme nerveux stable.

targets.query: SELECT 100 * stddev("avgOvernightHrv") / mean("avgOvernightHrv") AS "HRV CV %" FROM "SleepSummary" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(14d) fill(null)

panels.title: Climb rate par run (m/h)
panels.description: elevationGain / duration * 3600. Mesure la 'verticalité' moyenne de chaque run.

targets.query: SELECT ("elevationGain" / "elapsedDuration") * 3600 AS "Climb rate (m/h)" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "elevationGain" > 30 AND "elapsedDuration" > 600

panels.title: HR vs Pace agrégé (Z2, fenêtre)
panels.description: Scatter cross-activités sur la fenêtre — points en zone Z2 uniquement (HR 130-150 bpm typique). Le déplacement de la nuage vers la droite (mêmes BPM, vitesse plus haute) = base aérobie qui s'améliore.

targets.query: SELECT "Speed"*3.6 AS "Speed", "HeartRate" AS "HR" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "HeartRate" >= 130 AND "HeartRate" <= 150 AND $timeFilter

panels.title: Running Power Curve - Best mean power par duree (90j)
panels.description: Puissance moyenne maximale soutenue sur chaque duree dans les 90 derniers jours. Forme = equilibre des 3 systemes energetiques. Plateau 20-60min = Critical Power. Validator : voir si tes intensites produisent des PRs sur chaque bande.

targets.query: SELECT max("Power") FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/
SELECT max(mp) FROM (SELECT moving_average("Power", 5)    AS mp FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(mp) FROM (SELECT moving_average("Power", 30)   AS mp FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(mp) FROM (SELECT moving_average("Power", 60)   AS mp FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(mp) FROM (SELECT moving_average("Power", 300)  AS mp FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(mp) FROM (SELECT moving_average("Power", 1200) AS mp FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(mp) FROM (SELECT moving_average("Power", 3600) AS mp FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)

panels.title: Critical Pace Curve - Best mean speed par duree (90j)
panels.description: Vitesse moyenne maximale soutenue sur chaque duree. Plateau 20-60min = Critical Pace (FTPace running).

targets.query: SELECT max("Speed") * 3.6 FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/
SELECT max(ms) * 3.6 FROM (SELECT moving_average("Speed", 5)    AS ms FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(ms) * 3.6 FROM (SELECT moving_average("Speed", 30)   AS ms FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(ms) * 3.6 FROM (SELECT moving_average("Speed", 60)   AS ms FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(ms) * 3.6 FROM (SELECT moving_average("Speed", 300)  AS ms FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(ms) * 3.6 FROM (SELECT moving_average("Speed", 1200) AS ms FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)
SELECT max(ms) * 3.6 FROM (SELECT moving_average("Speed", 3600) AS ms FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE time > now() - 90d AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/)

panels.title: Efficiency Factor (NGP/HR) - per day
panels.description: GAP/HR median par jour. Plus haut = mieux. Indicateur Daniels d'efficacite aerobie (NGP rapportee a la FC). Sur 4-8 semaines a FC equivalente, monte = adaptations aerobies reelles. Plus fiable que le pace Z2 brut (moins biaise par composition warmup vs LR).

panels.title: 6. Pace @ FC fixée - cardiac efficiency progression
Pace @ FC fixée - 7 bins (120 → 180 ±5 bpm) - sorties plates (D+/km < 25)

targets.query: SELECT 1000 / "averageSpeed" AS "FC 120" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "averageHR" >= 115 AND "averageHR" <= 125 AND "distance" > 1000 AND "elevationGain" * 1000 < "distance" * 25

SELECT 1000 / "averageSpeed" AS "FC 130" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "averageHR" >= 125 AND "averageHR" <= 135 AND "distance" > 1000 AND "elevationGain" * 1000 < "distance" * 25

SELECT 1000 / "averageSpeed" AS "FC 140" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "averageHR" >= 135 AND "averageHR" <= 145 AND "distance" > 1000 AND "elevationGain" * 1000 < "distance" * 25

SELECT 1000 / "averageSpeed" AS "FC 150" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "averageHR" >= 145 AND "averageHR" <= 155 AND "distance" > 1000 AND "elevationGain" * 1000 < "distance" * 25

SELECT 1000 / "averageSpeed" AS "FC 160" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "averageHR" >= 155 AND "averageHR" <= 165 AND "distance" > 1000 AND "elevationGain" * 1000 < "distance" * 25

SELECT 1000 / "averageSpeed" AS "FC 170" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "averageHR" >= 165 AND "averageHR" <= 175 AND "distance" > 1000 AND "elevationGain" * 1000 < "distance" * 25

SELECT 1000 / "averageSpeed" AS "FC 180" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "averageHR" >= 175 AND "averageHR" <= 185 AND "distance" > 1000 AND "elevationGain" * 1000 < "distance" * 25

panels.description:
Pace @ FC fixee - Cardiac efficiency progression marker.

CE QUE CA MONTRE
Pour chaque sortie, on bin par FC moyenne en 7 plages (120, 130, ..., 180 ±5 bpm) et on plot la pace moyenne dans chaque bin. Sur 6 mois, l'amelioration de la base aerobie = pace plus rapide a FC constante.

LECTURE
- Y axis : pace en MM:SS / km (plus bas = plus rapide = mieux)
- 7 series = 7 bins de FC moyenne d'activite, gradient froid (bleu = bas FC) -> chaud (rouge = haut FC)
- Tendance descendante d'une serie = tu cours plus vite au meme effort cardio

POURQUOI C'EST PUISSANT
- Mesure directe des adaptations aerobies chroniques : volume ejection systolique, densification capillaire, hausse densite mitochondriale, economie de course.
- Plus interpretable qu'EF (un ratio) : '5:00/km a 150 bpm' parle plus que 'EF 0.40'.
- Independant des outils labo : extrait des sorties naturelles.

CALCUL
- Source : ActivitySummary (1 point par activite, lifetime via $timeFilter de 180j)
- Pace = 1000 / averageSpeed (m/s) en secondes/km
- Bin = activites ou averageHR in [FC-5, FC+5]
- Filtre terrain : elevationGain * 1000 < distance * 25 (= D+/km < 25 m/km, sorties plates pour eviter le biais terrain vallonne)
- Filtre distance : > 1 km
- Fenetre : 180 jours glissants

CAVEAT
- Une sortie 'intervalle' (ex 8x40s cotes) peut avoir averageHR ~140 mais une pace moyenne biaisee par l'alternance sprint/recup. En Z2 steady-state, bias faible.
- Source FC : toutes sources confondues (OHR poignet + ceinture HRM-Pro). L'OHR lag peut biaiser sur intervalles courts mais moins en steady-state.
- Filtre terrain restrictif : peu de sorties vallonnees passent < 25 m/km (verifier abondance des points).

APPLICATION
KPI principal de la base aerobie polarisee. Les series FC Z2 cible devraient descendre regulierement. Si pas de progression sur 8+ semaines a fenetre constante, signal : ajuster la stim (volume, intensite, repos).

title: 07 Sport-Science Validators
